Title: MdMTA-mediated m6A modification enhances drought tolerance by promoting mRNA stability and translation efficiency of genes involved in lignin deposition and oxidative stress

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Interference (RNAi) rather than complete knockout of MdMTA was used because strong loss-of-function appears lethal or non-propagable. MdMTA RNAi plants show more hypomethylated than hypermethylated m6A peaks, but the overall distribution of peaks across 5'UTR, CDS and 3'UTR is only modestly altered. MdMTA overexpression increases protein levels only about 2.2–2.4-fold over wild-type, which is sufficient to enhance drought tolerance but does not noticeably accelerate development.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MdMTA is an MTA-type m6A RNA methyltransferase in apple that forms a functional complex with MdMTB and catalyzes methyl group transfer to adenine in RNA probes containing the UGUAA/U motif.
2. MdMTA is localized in the nucleus and its expression is induced by drought and PEG-8000 treatment.
3. RNAi knockdown of MdMTA reduces global m6A levels in total RNA, causes widespread hypomethylation of mRNAs (especially in CDS and 3'UTR regions) and alters the transcriptome without a uniform direction of expression change.
4. MdMTA RNAi plants exhibit developmental defects (shorter plants, weaker roots) and lower lignin content and hydraulic conductivity, whereas MdMTA OE plants show increased lignin accumulation under drought.
5. Under drought, MdMTA positively regulates drought tolerance: RNAi plants show stronger wilting, higher ion leakage, reduced photosynthetic parameters and lower survival, while OE plants display the opposite phenotypes.
6. MdMTA-mediated m6A modification enhances the mRNA stability and, for selected targets, translation efficiency of genes involved in lignin biosynthesis and ROS scavenging, including MdLEA, MdLAC4, Md4CL3, MdORM2, MdMYC4, MdMPV17, MdCAT1, MdCAT2, MdPOD63 and MdPOD66.
7. Through these effects on target genes, MdMTA promotes lignin deposition, reduces H_2O_2 and MDA accumulation, and increases CAT and POD enzyme activities under drought, conferring enhanced drought tolerance in apple.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: m6A-seq and RNA-seq of GL-3 and MdMTA RNAi plants under normal and drought conditions; sequencing data deposited at NCBI under accession PRJNA772664.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Core m6A RNA methyltransferase that installs m6A on mRNAs and positively regulates drought tolerance by promoting lignin deposition and ROS scavenging; MdMTA knockdown reduces global m6A levels, impairs plant growth, lignification, hydraulic conductivity and antioxidant enzyme activities, and decreases drought survival, whereas overexpression increases m6A levels and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as the catalytic m6A writer within an MdMTA–MdMTB complex to install m6A on target mRNAs, mainly in CDS and 3'UTR and for some targets in the 5'UTR. Under drought, MdMTA-induced m6A modification generally enhances mRNA stability and for specific targets (e.g. Md4CL3 via 5'UTR m6A, MdLEA and MdMYC4 via CDS m6A, MdPOD63 via 5'UTR m6A) also increases translation efficiency, thereby raising protein output of drought- and ROS-related genes.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Central component of the m6A RNA modification pathway in apple that, under drought stress, increases m6A levels on transcripts involved in lignin biosynthesis (e.g. MdLAC4, Md4CL3) and ROS scavenging (e.g. MdLEA, MdORM2, MdMYC4, MdMPV17, MdCAT1, MdCAT2, MdPOD63, MdPOD66), thereby enhancing their expression and contributing to lignin deposition, antioxidant enzyme activity, reduced H_2O_2 and MDA accumulation, and improved drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: m6A mRNA methylation (RNA epigenetic modification pathway)
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: drought and PEG-8000 stress induce MdMTA expression. Downstream: MdMTA --(m6A methylation; activation via increased mRNA stability and/or translation under drought)--> MdLEA, MdLAC4, Md4CL3, MdORM2, MdMYC4, MdMPV17, MdCAT1, MdCAT2, MdPOD63 and MdPOD66, leading to enhanced lignin deposition and ROS scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MTA-type m6A RNA methyltransferase, orthologous to METTL3 in animals and closely related to Pyrus bretschneideri MTA.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, determined by MdMTA–GFP fusion localization.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdMTB (gene ID MD07G1276200); directly interacts with MdMTA as shown by Y2H and split-luciferase assays, and together with MdMTA is required in vitro to transfer the methyl group from SAM to adenine in RNA probes containing the UGUAA/U motif.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: MdMTA transcript levels are induced by drought and PEG-8000 treatments, showing time-dependent upregulation under these stress conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil drought stress (20–26 d withholding water followed by rewatering), PEG-8000-induced osmotic stress in hydroponic culture, and actinomycin D treatment to block transcription during drought for mRNA stability assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought tolerance, lignin deposition, oxidative stress and ROS scavenging, m6A RNA epigenetic regulation, and plant growth/development in apple.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought) and plant growth/root system development.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: MdMTA RNAi plants have reduced plant height and a weaker root system compared with GL-3, as well as decreased lignin content and lower root and shoot hydraulic conductivity, especially under prolonged growth; MdMTA OE plants resemble GL-3 in growth but show higher lignin content under drought.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Malus domestica GL-3 plants; MdMTA RNA interference (RNAi) lines (e.g. line #4) and MdMTA overexpression (OE) lines in the GL-3 background; 2–3-month-old potted plants grown in a glasshouse; hydroponically cultured 2-month-old plants for PEG treatment; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for split-luciferase and subcellular localization assays; recombinant MdMTA and MdMTB proteins expressed in Escherichia coli.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 (apple genotype used as wild-type and transgenic background)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of MdMTA overexpression and RNAi constructs into GL-3; m6A-IP and input library construction and sequencing (m6A-seq); RNA-seq; dot blot and LC-MS/MS quantification of m6A; in vitro methylation assays with recombinant MdMTA and MdMTB; Y2H and split-luciferase assays to test MdMTA–MdMTB interaction; subcellular localization by MdMTA–GFP fusion; drought treatment of potted plants and PEG-8000-induced osmotic stress; physiological measurements including survival rate, ion leakage, photosynthetic parameters, lignin content, hydraulic conductivity, H_2O_2 and MDA levels, and CAT/POD activities; m6A-IP-qPCR, qRT-PCR; actinomycin D chase assays for mRNA stability; polysome fractionation and qRT-PCR for translation efficiency; analysis of alternative splicing events from RNA-seq.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional analysis in transgenic apple GL-3 plants with MdMTA RNAi and overexpression; global m6A profiling by m6A-seq and LC-MS/MS; dot blot; drought and PEG stress assays; physiological measurements (survival, ion leakage, photosynthesis, lignin content, H_2O_2, MDA, CAT/POD activity); m6A-IP-qPCR, RNA-seq, mRNA stability assays with actinomycin D, and polysome-based translation efficiency assays; in vitro methylation assay together with MdMTB; Y2H and split-luciferase interaction assays; subcellular localization with GFP fusion.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: MdMTA was identified as the apple MTA ortholog by homology and phylogenetic analysis among plant MTA proteins, cloned from GL-3 cDNA, and functionally characterized as an m6A writer by in vitro methylation assays and by manipulating its expression in transgenic apple plants.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: MdMTA is a positive regulator of drought tolerance in apple; increasing MdMTA activity elevates global m6A levels, enhances lignin deposition and ROS scavenging, and improves survival, photosynthetic performance and membrane integrity under drought. MdMTA therefore represents a promising target gene for breeding or engineering apple cultivars with enhanced drought resistance via improved secondary cell wall formation and antioxidant capacity.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MdLEA is described as homologous to known LEA genes that positively regulate drought stress in other species, and in this study its regulation by MdMTA-mediated m6A is demonstrated at the RNA level, but MdLEA itself was not genetically overexpressed or knocked out in apple.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MdLEA is among the hypomethylated and downregulated genes in MdMTA RNAi plants under drought and is upregulated in MdMTA OE plants.
2. MdMTA-mediated m6A levels on MdLEA mRNA decrease in MdMTA RNAi and increase in OE plants under drought, as shown by m6A-IP-qPCR.
3. Under drought, MdLEA mRNA is less stable in MdMTA RNAi plants than in GL-3, whereas its stability is maintained in the presence of MdMTA activity.
4. The translation efficiency of MdLEA mRNA is also reduced in MdMTA RNAi plants compared with GL-3 under drought, indicating that MdMTA-mediated m6A enhances both mRNA stability and translation of MdLEA during drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Detected as an m6A-modified and differentially expressed transcript in the m6A-seq and RNA-seq datasets for MdMTA RNAi and GL-3 plants under drought (NCBI PRJNA772664).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdLEA mRNA is hypomethylated and downregulated in MdMTA RNAi plants and upregulated in MdMTA OE plants under drought; MdMTA-mediated m6A modification increases MdLEA mRNA stability and translation efficiency under drought, contributing to higher LEA transcript and protein levels during stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MdMTA-mediated m6A methylation on MdLEA transcripts (mainly in CDS/3'UTR regions) under drought slows MdLEA mRNA decay and increases its translation efficiency, leading to higher MdLEA transcript and protein abundance in MdMTA OE plants and lower levels in MdMTA RNAi plants.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Part of the MdMTA-dependent m6A regulatory pathway under drought; MdLEA acts as a stress-responsive LEA gene whose expression is enhanced by MdMTA-mediated m6A, contributing to cellular protection during drought and oxidative stress.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: MdMTA --(m6A methylation; activation)--> MdLEA under drought by increasing MdLEA m6A levels, mRNA stability and translation efficiency. Downstream: higher MdLEA expression is associated with enhanced drought response, consistent with LEA function reported in other species.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under drought, MdLEA expression is reduced in MdMTA RNAi plants and increased in MdMTA OE plants compared with GL-3; under normal conditions, only MdLEA shows a significant expression difference between genotypes among the tested targets.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (20 d soil drought) and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment for mRNA decay assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (drought and osmotic stress), m6A-mediated regulation of LEA gene expression in apple.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought and osmotic stress) and oxidative stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: GL-3 wild-type apple plants and MdMTA RNAi and OE transgenic plants subjected to drought and PEG-8000 treatments.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 background (MdMTA transgenic plants and wild-type GL-3)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: m6A-seq and RNA-seq to identify hypomethylated and differentially expressed transcripts; qRT-PCR to quantify MdLEA expression in GL-3, MdMTA RNAi and OE plants under normal and drought conditions; m6A-IP-qPCR to measure MdLEA m6A levels; actinomycin D chase assays under PEG-simulated drought for mRNA stability; polysome fractionation and qRT-PCR to determine translation efficiency.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identified as a hypomethylated DEG in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought by integrated m6A-seq and RNA-seq; expression confirmed by qRT-PCR; m6A enrichment validated by m6A-IP-qPCR; mRNA decay rate measured after PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment; translation efficiency assessed via polysome-associated vs total RNA under drought.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Selected from the set of hypomethylated transcripts that were also differentially expressed in MdMTA RNAi plants vs GL-3 under drought based on integrated m6A-seq and RNA-seq analyses, then validated by qRT-PCR and m6A-IP-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a drought-associated LEA gene whose m6A level, mRNA stability and translation efficiency are positively controlled by MdMTA under drought, MdLEA is a candidate target to enhance apple drought and osmotic stress tolerance by stabilizing stress-protective LEA transcripts, either via direct manipulation or through optimizing MdMTA-mediated m6A regulation.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The lignin-related function of LAC4 is supported by Arabidopsis data where lac4 mutants show reduced lignin content; in apple, MdLAC4 function is inferred from homology and from its MdMTA-dependent expression pattern and correlated lignin changes, but MdLAC4 itself was not genetically manipulated.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MdLAC4 is a hypomethylated, downregulated lignin-related transcript in MdMTA RNAi plants under drought and is upregulated in MdMTA OE plants.
2. m6A-IP-qPCR shows reduced m6A enrichment on MdLAC4 mRNA in MdMTA RNAi and increased enrichment in OE plants under drought.
3. MdLAC4 mRNA is less stable in MdMTA RNAi plants than in GL-3 under drought, indicating that MdMTA-mediated m6A promotes MdLAC4 transcript stability.
4. Increased MdLAC4 expression is consistent with higher lignin content observed in MdMTA OE plants under drought, linking MdLAC4 to MdMTA-dependent lignin deposition.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Detected as an m6A-modified, differentially expressed transcript in m6A-seq and RNA-seq datasets of MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought (PRJNA772664).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdLAC4 is hypomethylated and downregulated in MdMTA RNAi plants and upregulated in MdMTA OE plants under drought; MdMTA-mediated m6A methylation increases MdLAC4 mRNA stability in response to drought, supporting lignin deposition.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MdMTA-mediated m6A on MdLAC4 mRNA under drought increases its stability, leading to higher transcript accumulation in MdMTA OE plants and reduced levels in MdMTA RNAi plants, thereby supporting lignin biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Component of the MdMTA-controlled lignin biosynthetic pathway; m6A-dependent upregulation of MdLAC4 under drought contributes to increased lignin deposition and improved drought-associated hydraulic properties.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Lignin biosynthesis within the phenylpropanoid pathway.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: MdMTA --(m6A methylation; activation via increased mRNA stability under drought)--> MdLAC4. Downstream: elevated MdLAC4 expression contributes to lignin deposition, which is associated with improved drought tolerance in MdMTA OE plants.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: MdLAC4 transcript levels are lower in MdMTA RNAi plants and higher in MdMTA OE plants than in GL-3 under drought stress, while showing little difference under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (20 d) and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment for mRNA stability assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Lignin deposition and drought tolerance regulated via m6A-dependent control of lignin biosynthetic genes.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought) and lignin-related traits (secondary cell wall thickening and hydraulic conductivity).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: GL-3 and MdMTA RNAi and OE apple plants subjected to drought and PEG-8000 treatments for expression, m6A and stability assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 background (MdMTA transgenic and GL-3 plants)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: m6A-seq/RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; m6A-IP-qPCR; actinomycin D-based mRNA decay assays under PEG-simulated drought; lignin quantification in MdMTA transgenic and GL-3 plants to relate MdLAC4 expression to lignin content.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identified as a hypomethylated DEG from m6A-seq and RNA-seq in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought; MdLAC4 expression verified by qRT-PCR; m6A enrichment on MdLAC4 mRNA quantified by m6A-IP-qPCR; mRNA stability under PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment measured in MdMTA RNAi and GL-3 plants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Chosen from the group of hypomethylated and differentially expressed genes enriched in lignin biosynthetic processes in MdMTA RNAi plants under drought based on GO analysis of m6A-seq/RNA-seq data, then validated by qRT-PCR and m6A-IP-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because MdLAC4 is a lignin-associated gene whose m6A level and mRNA stability are positively regulated by MdMTA under drought, increasing MdLAC4 expression or optimizing its m6A regulation could enhance lignin deposition and improve hydraulic conductivity and drought tolerance in apple trees.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The role of 4CL enzymes in lignin biosynthesis is supported by previous work in other species; in apple, Md4CL3 is functionally implicated through MdMTA-dependent m6A regulation and translation changes but was not directly overexpressed or silenced.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Md4CL3 is a hypomethylated, downregulated lignin biosynthetic gene in MdMTA RNAi plants and is upregulated in MdMTA OE plants under drought.
2. m6A-IP-qPCR confirms that Md4CL3 mRNA has reduced m6A levels in MdMTA RNAi plants and increased levels in OE plants under drought.
3. mRNA stability assays show that Md4CL3 transcript decay is similar between MdMTA RNAi and GL-3 plants under drought, but translation efficiency is significantly reduced in MdMTA RNAi plants, consistent with m6A in its 5'UTR affecting translation.
4. These findings indicate that MdMTA enhances Md4CL3 protein production mainly by promoting translation, contributing to lignin deposition under drought.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Detected as an m6A-modified and differentially expressed transcript in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought in the m6A-seq/RNA-seq dataset (PRJNA772664).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Md4CL3 transcripts are hypomethylated and downregulated in MdMTA RNAi plants and upregulated in MdMTA OE plants under drought; MdMTA-mediated m6A in the 5'UTR promotes Md4CL3 translation efficiency under drought without markedly altering mRNA decay rate, supporting lignin biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MdMTA installs m6A in the 5'UTR of Md4CL3 mRNA under drought, and reduced m6A in MdMTA RNAi plants leads to decreased translation efficiency, while mRNA stability is largely unchanged, indicating that 5'UTR m6A mainly enhances translation of Md4CL3.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in the MdMTA-dependent lignin biosynthesis pathway; MdMTA-mediated 5'UTR m6A on Md4CL3 promotes its translation under drought, supporting increased lignin deposition and improved drought-associated water transport.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid and lignin biosynthesis (4-coumarate:CoA ligase step).
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: MdMTA --(5'UTR m6A methylation; activation by increasing translation under drought)--> Md4CL3. Downstream: elevated Md4CL3 protein contributes to lignin biosynthesis, supporting drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Md4CL3 transcript levels are lower in MdMTA RNAi plants and higher in MdMTA OE plants than in GL-3 under drought, with minimal differences under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (20 d) and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment for mRNA stability; drought conditions for translation efficiency assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Lignin deposition, phenylpropanoid metabolism and drought tolerance under m6A-dependent translational control.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought) and lignin-related traits affecting secondary cell wall formation and hydraulic conductivity.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: GL-3 and MdMTA RNAi and OE apple plants exposed to drought and PEG-8000 treatments.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 background (MdMTA transgenic and GL-3 plants)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: m6A-seq/RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; m6A-IP-qPCR; actinomycin D-based mRNA stability assays; polysome fractionation and qRT-PCR to measure translation efficiency; lignin content measurements in MdMTA transgenic plants.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identified as a hypomethylated, differentially expressed lignin biosynthetic gene via m6A-seq/RNA-seq in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought; validated by qRT-PCR and m6A-IP-qPCR; mRNA stability assessed by actinomycin D chase; translation efficiency determined by polysome association analysis, with Md4CL3 highlighted as having m6A in its 5'UTR that affects translation.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Selected from hypomethylated, differentially expressed genes enriched in lignin and phenylpropanoid biosynthetic processes in MdMTA RNAi plants under drought; validated by qRT-PCR and m6A-IP-qPCR, and its 5'UTR m6A position considered in designing translation assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Md4CL3, as a lignin biosynthetic gene with MdMTA-dependent m6A in its 5'UTR that enhances translation under drought, is a potential target to increase lignin deposition and improve drought-related water transport traits in apple.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The functional association of ORM2 with H_2O_2 regulation is based on Arabidopsis data; in apple, MdORM2 function is inferred from MdMTA-dependent m6A regulation and its drought-responsive expression pattern.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MdORM2 is a hypomethylated, downregulated H_2O_2-related gene in MdMTA RNAi plants under drought and is upregulated in MdMTA OE plants.
2. m6A-IP-qPCR shows reduced MdORM2 m6A enrichment in MdMTA RNAi and increased enrichment in OE plants under drought.
3. Under drought, MdORM2 mRNA decays faster in MdMTA RNAi than in GL-3 plants, indicating that MdMTA-mediated m6A promotes its mRNA stability.
4. Regulation of MdORM2 aligns with changes in ROS-related traits (H_2O_2 and MDA levels, antioxidant enzyme activity) observed between MdMTA genotypes.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Detected as an m6A-modified, differentially expressed transcript in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought (PRJNA772664).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdORM2 transcripts are hypomethylated and downregulated in MdMTA RNAi plants and upregulated in MdMTA OE plants under drought; MdMTA-mediated m6A increases MdORM2 mRNA stability during drought, supporting ROS-related protective functions.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MdMTA-mediated m6A modification on MdORM2 mRNA under drought stabilizes the transcript, resulting in higher MdORM2 expression in MdMTA OE plants and lower expression in MdMTA RNAi plants.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Part of the MdMTA-dependent ROS regulatory network; m6A-dependent upregulation of MdORM2 under drought likely contributes to reduced H_2O_2 accumulation and improved oxidative stress management.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Pathways related to H_2O_2 and ROS metabolism.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: MdMTA --(m6A methylation; activation via increased mRNA stability under drought)--> MdORM2. Downstream: elevated MdORM2 expression is linked to reduced H_2O_2 accumulation and enhanced drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under drought, MdORM2 expression is lower in MdMTA RNAi plants and higher in MdMTA OE plants than in GL-3, with little difference under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment for mRNA stability assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought-induced ROS metabolism and m6A-mediated regulation of H_2O_2-related genes.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought) and oxidative stress / ROS scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: GL-3 and MdMTA RNAi and OE apple plants subjected to drought and PEG-simulated drought.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 background (MdMTA transgenic and GL-3 plants)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: m6A-seq/RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; m6A-IP-qPCR; mRNA decay analysis following PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment under drought; physiological measurements of H_2O_2 and MDA content and antioxidant enzyme activities to relate gene regulation to ROS phenotypes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identified among hypomethylated DEGs related to H_2O_2 pathways from integrated m6A-seq and RNA-seq; validated by qRT-PCR and m6A-IP-qPCR; mRNA stability examined with PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 plants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Chosen from the intersection of hypomethylated peaks and DEGs enriched in H_2O_2-related pathways in MdMTA RNAi plants under drought, followed by validation via qRT-PCR and m6A-IP-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: MdORM2 is an H_2O_2-related gene whose m6A level and mRNA stability are positively controlled by MdMTA under drought; enhancing MdORM2 expression or its m6A regulation could contribute to improved ROS management and drought tolerance in apple.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis MYC4 overexpression increases anthocyanin, a nonenzymatic antioxidant; the apple MdMYC4 gene is considered functionally analogous, and its regulation by MdMTA-mediated m6A is demonstrated in this study.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MdMYC4 is a hypomethylated, downregulated transcript in MdMTA RNAi plants and is upregulated in MdMTA OE plants under drought.
2. m6A-IP-qPCR shows that MdMYC4 m6A levels are reduced in MdMTA RNAi and increased in OE plants under drought.
3. MdMYC4 mRNA is less stable and has reduced translation efficiency in MdMTA RNAi plants compared to GL-3 under drought, consistent with MdMTA-mediated CDS m6A promoting both stability and translation.
4. Regulation of MdMYC4 is linked to changes in ROS-related traits and is consistent with its known role in anthocyanin accumulation in other species.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Detected as an m6A-modified and differentially expressed transcript in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought (PRJNA772664).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdMYC4 is hypomethylated and downregulated in MdMTA RNAi plants and upregulated in MdMTA OE plants under drought; MdMTA-mediated m6A modification increases MdMYC4 mRNA stability and translation efficiency during drought, supporting anthocyanin accumulation and ROS scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MdMTA-mediated m6A in the CDS region of MdMYC4 enhances both mRNA stability and translation under drought; in MdMTA RNAi plants, decreased m6A leads to faster MdMYC4 mRNA decay and lower translation efficiency.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Part of the MdMTA-controlled ROS and antioxidant regulation pathway; MdMTA-induced MdMYC4 upregulation likely promotes anthocyanin accumulation and ROS scavenging during drought.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and ROS scavenging pathways.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: MdMTA --(m6A methylation in CDS; activation via increased mRNA stability and translation under drought)--> MdMYC4. Downstream: increased MdMYC4 expression is associated with enhanced anthocyanin-based ROS scavenging during drought.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under drought, MdMYC4 expression is lower in MdMTA RNAi plants and higher in MdMTA OE plants compared with GL-3, with minimal differences under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (20 d) and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D for stability assays; drought for translation efficiency assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought-induced anthocyanin and ROS scavenging regulated by m6A-dependent control of a MYC transcription factor.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought) and oxidative stress / antioxidant (anthocyanin-mediated) response.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: GL-3 and MdMTA RNAi and OE apple plants under drought and PEG-simulated drought.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 background (MdMTA transgenic and GL-3 plants)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: m6A-seq/RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; m6A-IP-qPCR; actinomycin D chase for mRNA stability; polysome fractionation and qRT-PCR for translation efficiency; ROS-related phenotyping (H_2O_2 and MDA levels, antioxidant enzyme activities).
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identified as a hypomethylated, differentially expressed gene from m6A-seq/RNA-seq; validated by qRT-PCR and m6A-IP-qPCR; mRNA stability measured with PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D; translation efficiency measured via polysome association, with MdMYC4 indicated as a CDS-methylated transcript whose translation efficiency is reduced in MdMTA RNAi plants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Selected from hypomethylated DEGs enriched in H_2O_2 and anthocyanin-related pathways in MdMTA RNAi plants under drought; validated using qRT-PCR and m6A-IP-qPCR and characterized by stability and translation assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: MdMYC4, regulated at the m6A, stability and translation levels by MdMTA under drought, may serve as a target to enhance anthocyanin-based antioxidant capacity and drought tolerance in apple.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Loss of MPV17 function in other species increases ROS levels, supporting its role in ROS metabolism; in apple, MdMPV17 is linked to ROS regulation through MdMTA-dependent m6A and expression changes.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MdMPV17 is a hypomethylated, downregulated ROS-related gene in MdMTA RNAi plants and is upregulated in MdMTA OE plants under drought.
2. m6A-IP-qPCR reveals that MdMPV17 m6A levels decrease in MdMTA RNAi and increase in OE plants during drought.
3. MdMPV17 mRNA is less stable in MdMTA RNAi plants, indicating that MdMTA-mediated m6A promotes its transcript stability under drought.
4. The regulation of MdMPV17 coincides with changes in ROS-related phenotypes between MdMTA genotypes, suggesting a role in drought-associated ROS metabolism.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Identified as an m6A-modified, differentially expressed transcript in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought (PRJNA772664).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdMPV17 is hypomethylated and downregulated in MdMTA RNAi plants and upregulated in MdMTA OE plants under drought; MdMTA-mediated m6A modification increases MdMPV17 mRNA stability in drought conditions, supporting peroxisomal ROS metabolism.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MdMTA-mediated m6A on MdMPV17 mRNA under drought stabilizes the transcript; reduced m6A in MdMTA RNAi plants leads to faster decay and lower MdMPV17 mRNA levels.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in the MdMTA-dependent ROS scavenging network; increased MdMPV17 expression contributes to peroxisomal ROS metabolism, aiding in H_2O_2 detoxification and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Peroxisomal ROS metabolism and reactive oxygen detoxification pathways.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: MdMTA --(m6A methylation; activation via increased mRNA stability under drought)--> MdMPV17. Downstream: higher MdMPV17 expression is associated with reduced ROS accumulation and improved drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under drought, MdMPV17 expression is reduced in MdMTA RNAi plants and elevated in MdMTA OE plants compared with GL-3.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (20 d) and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D for stability assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought-induced ROS metabolism and peroxisome-associated oxidative stress responses under m6A regulation.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought) and oxidative stress / ROS scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: GL-3 and MdMTA RNAi and OE apple plants subjected to drought and PEG-8000 treatment.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 background (MdMTA transgenic and GL-3 plants)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: m6A-seq/RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; m6A-IP-qPCR; actinomycin D chase mRNA stability assays; ROS-related physiological measurements (H_2O_2, MDA, CAT/POD activities) to connect MdMPV17 regulation with oxidative stress phenotypes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identified as a hypomethylated, differentially expressed gene related to ROS metabolism in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought by m6A-seq/RNA-seq; validated via qRT-PCR and m6A-IP-qPCR; mRNA stability tested using PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D in MdMTA RNAi and GL-3 plants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Selected from hypomethylated DEGs enriched in H_2O_2 and ROS-related processes in MdMTA RNAi plants under drought, then validated by qRT-PCR and m6A-IP-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: MdMPV17, whose m6A level and mRNA stability are positively regulated by MdMTA under drought, could be exploited, together with MdMTA, to improve peroxisome-associated ROS metabolism and drought tolerance in apple.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MdCAT1 m6A peaks were not detected in the current m6A-seq dataset but were identified in previously published m6A-seq data; these prior sites guided m6A-IP-qPCR primer design. m6A levels on MdCAT1 differ among genotypes only under drought, not under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MdCAT1 expression is reduced in MdMTA RNAi plants and increased in MdMTA OE plants under drought, paralleling changes in catalase activity.
2. m6A-IP-qPCR confirms that MdCAT1 mRNA carries MdMTA-dependent m6A modifications under drought (lower in RNAi, higher in OE), whereas m6A levels are similar among genotypes under control conditions.
3. MdCAT1 mRNA stability is lower in MdMTA RNAi plants than in GL-3 under drought, while translation efficiency is not significantly affected.
4. These regulatory effects align with higher H_2O_2 and MDA levels and lower CAT activity in MdMTA RNAi plants, implicating MdCAT1 in MdMTA-mediated ROS scavenging during drought.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Differential expression detected in RNA-seq of MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought (PRJNA772664); m6A modification sites supported by previous m6A-seq datasets (Mao et al., 2021).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Under drought, MdCAT1 expression is downregulated in MdMTA RNAi plants and upregulated in MdMTA OE plants; MdMTA-mediated m6A modification increases MdCAT1 mRNA stability (with translation efficiency largely unchanged), contributing to higher catalase activity and lower H_2O_2 levels.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MdMTA-mediated m6A modification on MdCAT1 mRNA under drought enhances mRNA stability, leading to higher MdCAT1 transcript abundance in MdMTA OE plants and lower levels in MdMTA RNAi plants; translation efficiency is not significantly altered.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Part of the MdMTA-controlled antioxidant defense pathway; upregulation of MdCAT1 via m6A-dependent mRNA stabilization contributes to increased catalase activity, reduced H_2O_2 accumulation and improved drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Antioxidant defense pathway, catalase-mediated H_2O_2 detoxification.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: MdMTA --(m6A methylation; activation via increased mRNA stability under drought)--> MdCAT1. Downstream: higher MdCAT1 expression enhances catalase-mediated H_2O_2 scavenging and contributes to drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: MdCAT1 transcript levels are lower in MdMTA RNAi plants and higher in MdMTA OE plants than in GL-3 under drought, while showing no significant differences among genotypes under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought treatment for 20 d; PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment for mRNA stability; drought for translation efficiency assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought-induced antioxidant defense, catalase activity and m6A-dependent regulation of H_2O_2 scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought) and oxidative stress / ROS scavenging (catalase activity).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: GL-3 and MdMTA RNAi and OE apple plants subjected to drought and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatments.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 background (MdMTA transgenic and GL-3 plants)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA-seq to identify differentially expressed antioxidant enzyme genes; qRT-PCR to validate MdCAT1 expression; m6A-IP-qPCR to quantify MdCAT1 m6A levels; actinomycin D-based mRNA decay assays under drought; polysome fractionation and qRT-PCR for translation efficiency; biochemical assays for H_2O_2, MDA, CAT and POD activities.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: MdCAT1 was selected based on altered expression in RNA-seq of MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought; expression validated by qRT-PCR; m6A modification confirmed by m6A-IP-qPCR using primers designed from previous m6A-seq datasets; mRNA stability measured by actinomycin D chase under drought; translation efficiency assessed via polysome profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Chosen from antioxidant enzyme-encoding genes with altered expression in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought; m6A sites inferred from previous m6A-seq datasets and used to design m6A-IP-qPCR primers.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: MdCAT1 encodes a catalase involved in H_2O_2 scavenging whose m6A level, mRNA stability and expression are positively controlled by MdMTA under drought; enhancing MdCAT1 expression or its m6A regulation could increase catalase activity, reduce oxidative damage and improve drought tolerance in apple.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MdCAT2 m6A peaks were not identified in the current m6A-seq dataset but are supported by previously published m6A-seq data; m6A differences among genotypes appear specifically under drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MdCAT2 expression is downregulated in MdMTA RNAi and upregulated in MdMTA OE plants under drought.
2. m6A-IP-qPCR shows that MdCAT2 transcripts carry MdMTA-dependent m6A marks under drought, with lower levels in RNAi and higher in OE plants, and no significant genotype differences under control conditions.
3. MdCAT2 mRNA stability is reduced in MdMTA RNAi plants under drought, while translation efficiency remains similar to GL-3.
4. These effects support a role for MdCAT2 in MdMTA-mediated enhancement of catalase activity and ROS detoxification during drought.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Differentially expressed in RNA-seq of MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought (PRJNA772664); m6A modification confirmed using prior m6A-seq data and m6A-IP-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdCAT2 expression is reduced in MdMTA RNAi plants and increased in MdMTA OE plants under drought; MdMTA-mediated m6A increases MdCAT2 mRNA stability (without markedly altering translation efficiency), contributing to overall catalase activity and ROS scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MdMTA-mediated m6A on MdCAT2 mRNA boosts its stability under drought; translation efficiency is not significantly impacted, indicating that m6A mainly affects transcript longevity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Within the MdMTA-dependent antioxidant defense pathway, MdCAT2 upregulation via m6A-dependent mRNA stabilization contributes to increased catalase activity and reduced oxidative damage under drought.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Antioxidant defense pathway, catalase-mediated H_2O_2 detoxification.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: MdMTA --(m6A methylation; activation via increased mRNA stability under drought)--> MdCAT2. Downstream: increased MdCAT2 expression contributes to catalase-mediated H_2O_2 removal and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: MdCAT2 transcript levels are lower in MdMTA RNAi plants and higher in MdMTA OE plants than in GL-3 under drought, with no significant differences under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (20 d) and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment for stability assays; drought for translation efficiency assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought-induced antioxidant defense, catalase activity and m6A-dependent regulation of H_2O_2 scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought) and oxidative stress / ROS scavenging (catalase activity).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: GL-3 and MdMTA RNAi and OE apple plants subjected to drought and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 background (MdMTA transgenic and GL-3 plants)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; m6A-IP-qPCR; actinomycin D-based mRNA stability assays; polysome-based translation efficiency assays; measurement of H_2O_2, MDA, CAT and POD activities.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identified as a differentially expressed catalase gene in RNA-seq of MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought; expression validated via qRT-PCR; m6A levels determined by m6A-IP-qPCR informed by prior m6A-seq; mRNA stability measured by actinomycin D chase; translation efficiency measured by polysome profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Selected from MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 RNA-seq as a catalase-encoding DEG under drought; m6A target sites inferred from previous m6A-seq and used for m6A-IP-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: MdCAT2, like MdCAT1, is a catalase gene whose drought-induced expression and mRNA stability depend on MdMTA-mediated m6A; it is therefore a potential target for improving catalase capacity and drought tolerance in apple through modulation of m6A regulation or gene expression.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MdPOD63 m6A marks were not detected in the current m6A-seq dataset but are supported by previous m6A-seq data; only its 5'UTR m6A is reported to significantly affect translation efficiency under drought.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MdPOD63 expression is reduced in MdMTA RNAi and increased in MdMTA OE plants under drought, paralleling changes in POD activity.
2. m6A-IP-qPCR demonstrates MdMTA-dependent m6A enrichment on MdPOD63 mRNA under drought (lower in RNAi, higher in OE) but not under control conditions.
3. MdPOD63 mRNA stability is lower in MdMTA RNAi plants, and its translation efficiency is significantly reduced compared with GL-3 under drought, consistent with functional m6A in the 5'UTR.
4. These data support a role for MdPOD63 as an MdMTA-regulated peroxidase contributing to ROS detoxification and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Differential expression detected in RNA-seq of MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought (PRJNA772664); m6A modification validated using previous m6A-seq data and m6A-IP-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdPOD63 expression is reduced in MdMTA RNAi plants and increased in MdMTA OE plants under drought; MdMTA-mediated m6A modification (including in the 5'UTR) increases both MdPOD63 mRNA stability and translation efficiency during drought, supporting higher POD activity and improved ROS scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MdMTA-mediated m6A on MdPOD63 mRNA, including in the 5'UTR, enhances both mRNA stability and translation efficiency under drought; loss of MdMTA reduces m6A, accelerates mRNA decay and decreases translation.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Within the MdMTA-dependent antioxidant defense network, MdPOD63 upregulation via m6A-dependent increases in mRNA stability and translation contributes to elevated peroxidase activity, lower H_2O_2 and MDA levels and enhanced drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Antioxidant defense pathway, peroxidase-mediated H_2O_2 scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: MdMTA --(m6A methylation in 5'UTR and other regions; activation via increased mRNA stability and translation under drought)--> MdPOD63. Downstream: increased MdPOD63 expression enhances POD activity and supports ROS removal and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: MdPOD63 transcript levels are lower in MdMTA RNAi plants and higher in MdMTA OE plants than in GL-3 under drought, with similar expression under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought treatment for 20 d; PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D for stability assays; drought for translation efficiency assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought-induced peroxidase activity, ROS scavenging and m6A-mediated regulation of POD gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought) and oxidative stress / ROS scavenging (peroxidase activity).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: GL-3 and MdMTA RNAi and OE apple plants under drought and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 background (MdMTA transgenic and GL-3 plants)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; m6A-IP-qPCR; actinomycin D-based mRNA stability assays; polysome-based translation efficiency measurements; measurement of H_2O_2, MDA, CAT and POD activities.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Identified as a peroxidase-encoding DEG in RNA-seq of MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought; expression validated by qRT-PCR; m6A levels measured by m6A-IP-qPCR using primers designed from previous m6A-seq data; mRNA stability assessed with actinomycin D chase under drought; translation efficiency quantified via polysome profiling, with MdPOD63 highlighted as having 5'UTR m6A affecting translation.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Selected from peroxidase-encoding genes that are differentially expressed in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought; m6A target positions derived from previous m6A-seq archived data and used to design m6A-IP-qPCR primers.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: MdPOD63 encodes a peroxidase whose m6A level, mRNA stability and 5'UTR-dependent translation efficiency are positively regulated by MdMTA under drought; manipulating MdPOD63 or its m6A regulation could increase POD activity, improve ROS detoxification and enhance drought tolerance in apple.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MdPOD66 m6A marks were not detected in the current m6A-seq experiment but were supported by earlier m6A-seq datasets; translation efficiency of MdPOD66 is not significantly changed by MdMTA knockdown under drought, indicating a primary effect on mRNA stability.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MdPOD66 expression is lowered in MdMTA RNAi and elevated in MdMTA OE plants under drought in parallel with changes in POD activity.
2. m6A-IP-qPCR shows MdMTA-dependent m6A enrichment on MdPOD66 mRNA under drought, with no significant genotype differences under control conditions.
3. MdPOD66 mRNA decays faster in MdMTA RNAi plants than in GL-3 under drought, whereas its translation efficiency remains similar between genotypes.
4. These findings support MdPOD66 as an MdMTA-regulated peroxidase gene contributing to ROS detoxification and drought tolerance primarily through mRNA stabilization.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Differentially expressed in RNA-seq of MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought (PRJNA772664); m6A modification verified by m6A-IP-qPCR guided by prior m6A-seq.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdPOD66 expression is reduced in MdMTA RNAi plants and increased in MdMTA OE plants under drought; MdMTA-mediated m6A increases MdPOD66 mRNA stability, while translation efficiency remains largely unchanged, contributing to total peroxidase activity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MdMTA-mediated m6A on MdPOD66 mRNA under drought enhances transcript stability; translation efficiency is not detectably altered, suggesting that m6A mainly affects decay rather than translation for this gene.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Part of the MdMTA-controlled antioxidant defense network; MdPOD66 upregulation via m6A-enhanced mRNA stability contributes to total peroxidase activity and H_2O_2 scavenging under drought.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Antioxidant defense pathway, peroxidase-mediated H_2O_2 scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: MdMTA --(m6A methylation; activation via increased mRNA stability under drought)--> MdPOD66. Downstream: higher MdPOD66 expression supports POD activity, reducing oxidative damage and contributing to drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: MdPOD66 transcript levels are lower in MdMTA RNAi plants and higher in MdMTA OE plants relative to GL-3 under drought; expression differences are not evident under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (20 d) and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D for mRNA stability; drought for translation efficiency assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought-induced peroxidase activity, ROS scavenging and m6A-dependent regulation of POD gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought) and oxidative stress / ROS scavenging (peroxidase activity).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: GL-3 and MdMTA RNAi and OE apple plants under drought and PEG-8000 plus actinomycin D treatment.
Plant_Genes.Variety: GL-3 background (MdMTA transgenic and GL-3 plants)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; m6A-IP-qPCR; actinomycin D-based mRNA stability assays; polysome translation efficiency assays; biochemical assays for H_2O_2, MDA, CAT and POD activities.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Selected as a peroxidase-encoding DEG from RNA-seq; expression validated by qRT-PCR; m6A modification confirmed by m6A-IP-qPCR based on previous m6A-seq data; mRNA stability analyzed with actinomycin D chase; translation efficiency measured by polysome profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Chosen from peroxidase-encoding genes with differential expression in MdMTA RNAi vs GL-3 under drought; putative m6A sites from previous m6A-seq informed m6A-IP-qPCR design.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: MdPOD66 is a peroxidase whose m6A level and mRNA stability are positively regulated by MdMTA under drought; its manipulation, along with MdMTA, may enhance peroxidase activity, reduce oxidative damage and improve drought tolerance in apple.
